entry.title: SAP BTP API Proxy to Work with Basic Authentication or OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
Introduction

In modern SAP integration landscapes, organizations often face diverse authentication requirements. Some external consumers may depend on Basic Authentication due to legacy system constraints or simplicity, while others are required to use OAuth 2.0 to comply with enterprise security policies.
In this blog post, we’ll explore how to configure a single SAP BTP API Management proxy that supports both Basic Authentication and OAuth 2.0, ensuring flexibility for a range of API consumers without compromising on security standards

Business Scenario

In complex enterprise environments, particularly federated organizations:

  • Legacy systems or subsidiaries may still utilize Basic Authentication.
  • Corporate IT may mandate OAuth 2.0 for cloud and mobile integrations.
  •  
  • Maintaining separate API proxies for each authentication method is undesirable due to increased complexity and maintenance.

Objective: Implement a unified API proxy in SAP BTP that dynamically supports both authentication methods based on the incoming request.

Solution Architecture

Here's how the solution works:

API Consumer --> SAP BTP API Proxy (auth policy check) --> SAP Backend (e.g., S/4HANA)

Various consumers, such as Legacy, Cloud, or Mobile applications, may utilize the same proxy endpoint with either Basic or OAuth2.0 authentication. The proxy inspects the request for:

  • Authorization: If "Basic", it validates via Basic Authentication.
  • Authorization: If "Bearer", it validates via the OAuth 2.0 token.
  • Upon successful authentication, the request is routed to the backend.
  • If authentication fails, a response is sent back.
Implementation Steps
  • Develop an API Proxy in SAP BTP API Management, ensuring to establish the backend SAP connection for oData services.

     

Required Parameters will be entered.

  • API Basepath:
  • Target Endpoint:
    • API Provider: SAP (or any provider)
    • URL: Odata path (or any URL)
  • Add Pre-Flow Policies with condition routing.

Please refer to the following for more detailed information on each step.

  • RaiseFault: This step checks if authentication is used and raises a fault if neither Basic nor OAuth is applied.
    • Condtion String  request.header.authorization = null
    • Policy String:

  • oAuthVerify: This function verifies oAuth credentials when a bearer token is provided.
    • Condition String  request.header.Authorization ~ "Bearer*"
    • Policy String:

  • decodebasicAuth: Decodes Basic Authentication.
    • Condition String : request.header.Authorization ~ "Basic*".
    • Policy String :

 

  • verifyKey: This will check if the key is associated with this API Product APP key.
    • Consdition String : request.header.Authorization !~ "Bearer*"
    • Policy String :

 

  • invalidCredentials: This fault exception occurs when credential validation fails.
    • Condition String : (verifyapikey.Verify-API-Key.failed = true) or (varanganti.clientSecret != verifyapikey.verifyKey.client_secret)
    • Policy String:

  • RemoveHeader (Optional): This function removes the API headers post-authorization, as they are no longer necessary for targeting the application once authorization is successful. The necessity of this step depends on subsequent procedures.
  • Readkey (Optional): Since I am accessing an OData service on the backend, credentials for Key Vault Manager (KVM) are being read.
  • Basic (Optional): Include a Basic Header for the target application if necessary. This depends on how the target key is being accepted.
  • scCSRF (Optional): This parameter is used to set the CSRF token, as is required by my target application.
  • setSCParsms (Optional) :  Follow-up step for SCRF to call backend application.
  • Configure Credential Aliases

In the API Developer Hub, create an application to obtain credentials. These will be utilized for basic authentication or OAuth Authentication.

  • Testing the Proxy
    • Cas1: No Authentication
      • Do Not pass any credentials

 

  • Cas2: Basic Authentication
    • The call will succeed and receive a response.

 

  • Case 3: Utilizing OAuth Authentication
    • Pre-Requisite: Set up a proxy with a placeholder target for obtaining the OAuth authentication token.
    • Select OAuth2.0 as the authentication method and execute accordingly.

 

Conclusion

Configuring a single SAP BTP API proxy to support both Basic Authentication and OAuth 2.0 provides a scalable and secure solution for diverse integration requirements. This approach minimizes redundancy, simplifies management, and supports both modern and legacy applications accessing the same backend services.

By leveraging policy-based routing and dynamic authentication handling, enterprises can ensure compliance, enhance flexibility, and optimize their API architecture.

entry.title: What Really Happens After cf push? A Peek Inside SAP BTP’s Engine Room
entry.content:
As developers, we rarely settle for just seeing the finished product. Whether it's a blockbuster movie or a powerful software application, we naturally want to know how it all comes together behind the scenes. We think beyond the user interface and final output — we want to understand the entire process, the components involved, and the magic that brings it to life.

When you deploy an app to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) using Cloud Foundry (CF), a lot happens after you run a simple command like:

cf push my-app

This blog post takes you on a journey through the backstage of your app deployment, revealing the key steps Cloud Foundry performs to transform your code into a live, running application in the cloud.

1. Upload and Package Your App
When you push your app, Cloud Foundry compresses all your application files into a package and uploads it to the Cloud Controller. This component manages the deployment process and stores metadata about your app.

2. Staging – Preparing Your App to Run
Cloud Foundry then creates a temporary Staging Container where your app is prepared to run. It selects a suitable Buildpack — a set of scripts tailored for your app’s language or framework (Node.js, Java, Python, etc.). The buildpack installs necessary runtimes and dependencies (like npm install for Node.js), and then creates a runnable image called a droplet.

3. Droplet Storage
Once the droplet is created, it is stored securely in the Blobstore, a central file storage system. The Cloud Controller updates its records with the new droplet information.

4. App Launch
Cloud Foundry then assigns a Diego Cell (a container host) to run your app. Diego downloads the droplet and runs it inside a secure Linux container (called a Garden container). It starts your app’s main process (for example, npm start or java -jar app.jar).

5. Routing – Making Your App Accessible
Your app is assigned a URL (like my-app.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com). The Router component listens for incoming requests on this URL and forwards them to your running container, so users can access your application from anywhere.

6. Logging and Monitoring
Logs generated by your app are streamed to Loggregator, which you can monitor in real-time using commands like:

cf logs my-app --recent

Cloud Foundry’s Health Manager continuously checks your app’s health, restarts it if it crashes, and scales it according to demand.

7. Ongoing Maintenance
Need to handle more users? Simply scale your app instances with:

cf scale my-app -i 3

Want to deploy updates? Just run cf push again to push a new version without downtime.

In Summary:
Behind that simple cf push command, Cloud Foundry orchestrates a complex but efficient process — from packaging your code, staging it, running it securely in containers, routing traffic, to monitoring and scaling your app seamlessly. This automation frees developers to focus on building great apps without worrying about infrastructure management.

Understanding this process helps you better design, troubleshoot, and optimize your applications on SAP BTP.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors User Groups | updates

entry.title: Palestras realizadas #SITREC2024
entry.content:
O SAP Inside Track Recife 2024 (Blog do evento) foi um marco na retomada dos eventos presenciais da comunidade SAP em Recife, após um intervalo desde 2020. Realizado no dia 5 de outubro nas dependências da Uninassau, o evento reuniu cerca de 150 profissionais da área, com todos os 100 ingressos disponibilizados ao público geral esgotados com semanas de antecedência.

Com o apoio de patrocinadores como MakeValue, GFX, Tachyonix, Accenture e Moovi Education, o evento foi um marco de engajamento, aprendizado e conexão entre profissionais SAP. A abertura ficou por conta de uma apresentação cultural com orquestra de frevo, passistas e até mesmo um robô dançando ao som da música tradicional pernambucana.

A agenda reuniu palestras de alto nível, com temas atuais e práticos, mantiveram o auditório cheio até o encerramento, que contou com sorteios de brindes e muito networking. A seguir, uma retrospectiva das palestras apresentadas:

 

Título da PalestraNome do(s) Palestrante(s)

Abertura - Key NoteCláudia Daniele, @Marssel700 , @marco_v_santos 
Maximizando o Valor do S/4HANA@Marssel700 
Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence: Revolucionando a Gestão de Crédito@Jonys_Arcanjo 
Clean Core, SAP BTP e como a Tachyonix é a peça que faltava nesse quebra-cabeçaLucas Guedes Ferreira
Business innovation challenges and impacts for S/4HANA@MRobalinho 
Facilita com BTP@afigueredo54 e João Paulo Cabral
GFX Pagamento com cartões integrado ao S/4HANA Public EditionFrederico Figueiredo
Desmitificando o SAP DRC NFe - Como se preparar para uma migração?@danilo_bispo 
Inovação em SAP: Explorando Tecnologias Emergentes@JOGethmann e Alan Dias
Encerramento - SorteioEquipe de Organização SITREC

Podcast

  • Episódio 01 - Clean Core com SAP BTP

  • Episódio 02 - Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence

Vídeos de edições anteriores: 2025 2024 2020 2017 2016

entry.title: Re: ListPicker items doesn't show all the information in...

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors User Groups | updates

entry.title: I would like to understand how the Userld works within Emarsys
entry.content:
I would like to understand how the Userld works within Emarsys, how it is generated and what the difference is between the Userld and the ExternallD available in the platform.

We are currently experiencing issues with the abandoned cart journey. There is a significant delay between the cart abandonment and the communication.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: I would like to understand how the Userld works withi...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

Explaining the difference between the IDs you requested:

  • userid is also known as uid or Emarsys unique id -> this ID is automatically generated by the system and is always unique. This is used as an internal identifier in Emarsys backend to ensure uniqueness to each contact, despite of the unique identifier set by the customer when importing the contact via CSV, or the Web Extend unique identifier, for example. The userid cannot be changed and is always manipulated by Emarsys itself.
  • External ID is another ID, completely independent of the userid -> this ID can be any custom field of your choice. It is a generic name to address any custom field that the customer chooses as the unique identifier for their contact imports, Web Extend and Smart Insight. Usually customers choose their unique identifier based on an ID they already use on their other integrated systems.

 

For abandoned cart, which uses Web Extend events, you can choose to use email or an external ID as the unique identifier. The userid is not involved in the scripts as it is handled by Emarsys itself. There are some scenarios that you may see and use the userid, like some API endpoints and Open Data. The naming can be a little confusing in the Web Extend documentation for example, where the external ID is referenced in some pages as "setCustomerId" but this is also the external ID that you have requested SAP to set up as the unique identifier for this purpose when the account was in the set up stage.

 

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: MDK Grid table converting into Object page on iPad Ai...

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP API Proxy to Work with Basic Authentication or OAuth 2.0

Very good article.

What credentials shall we create and give for oAuth2 in the policy.

Thanks,

Ikram.

entry.title: Planned Integration of TM and WM (5HP/1P9/BME + 3BS) – How to Track Upcoming Releases?
entry.content:
In the public cloud we are planning to implement warehouses, which are WM managed. In the outbound process (scope ID 3BS) there is also a need to plan the transports for stock transfer orders. We would like to use the scope IDs 5HP, 1P9 and BME for country internal and cross border stock transfers. We have found out, that this scenario is not supported, but there is something planned for 2026 Q1. Can you please share a list of functionalities, which are not supported so far (like delivery creation via transport cockpit? automatically posting GI via check out etc?). Can you please also share a detailed list, about which functionalities will be included in the release in Q1 2026? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Planned Integration of TM and WM (5HP/1P9/BME + 3BS)...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Currently, the transportation planning scenario for stock transfer orders (STOs) using scope items 5HP, 1P9, and BME in combination with WM-managed warehouses (scope item 3BS) is not yet supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. Key functionalities such as delivery creation via the transportation cockpit, automatic integration with freight units, and automatic posting of goods issue (GI) after checkout are not available. However, SAP has created a plan for the Q1 2026 release, aiming to enable a fully integrated and automated end-to-end process for internal and cross-border STOs, including seamless integration with the transportation cockpit and improved logistical execution.

Customers can explore upcoming innovations and planned features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition through the following official channels: 

  • SAP Road Map Explorer
    This tool provides a detailed view of planned and released innovations across SAP products. Users can filter by product, industry, region, and timeline.
    🔗 https://roadmaps.sap.com

For your case, I have already select the filter for the next releases that include 2026 Q1 as requested to see all the planned features for this release for Supply Chain line of business: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?BC=6EAE8B27FCC11ED892E91B6B24A440CC&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q4%202025

For next platforms, please note that for release 2026 Q1, it may arrive full details close to the planned release. 

  • SAP Help Portal – What’s New Viewer
    Shows release-specific highlights and detailed updates by version (e.g., 2408 2502), including innovations per line of business.
    🔗 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/what_s_new

  • SAP Community – S/4HANA Cloud Tag
    Blog posts, Q&As, and expert insights often include information about upcoming changes and real-life implementation feedback.
    🔗 https://community.sap.com/topics/s4hana-cloud

  • SAP for Me Portal – Product and Maintenance Information
    Provides personalized product information, including release schedules, support timelines, and maintenance windows.
    🔗 https://me.sap.com

  • Release Assessment and Scope Dependency Tool (RASD) (for customers only)
    This internal tool helps customers assess changes coming in the next release based on their system usage.
    🔗 Access via SAP for Me, under "Systems & Provisioning" > "Release Information"

Thank you very much!

Best regards,

Geovana

entry.title: Get trail mail for audit log of BOM in S4HC?
entry.content:
We need audit trails for the internal audit team. And one of the requirement is change log for BOM's.
Kindly let us know how this dump can be accessed?

*Note - Change versions for BOM are not maintained


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Get trail mail for audit log of BOM in S4HC?
entry.content:
Please refer to the KBA  https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3091633 and section :

The app 'Display Change Documents' ( App Id CA_SHOW_CD ) allows the viewing of the change documents from CDHDR/CDPOS

If you run it with Change Document Object : STUE it will show you the change documents for BOM's

entry.title: Re: Depreciation calculation based on net book value
entry.content:
HI@AmitavaSaha 

For your requirement, you can use depreciation key LINS or copy LINS and create Z* Depreciation key. 

Depreciation key LINS calculation is based on NBV and remaining useful life.

NOTE - Don't worry about LINS is straight line method. It will give result as per WDV remaining useful life only as calculation results will be same for both. You can test in Dev or Quality for checking, 

Just  check and confirm. This should work as per your requirement.

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

Please let me know if you have any questions. Kind Regards Ajit

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP API Proxy to Work with Basic Authentication or OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
Hi Ikram, 

I hope your question is about creating basic authentication credentials and understanding what needs to be used as the client ID and secret for OAuth to work. If that's the case, then the answer is that the same basic authentication credentials (assuming you have created the credentials app in the developer hub) will function as the OAuth client and secret.

 

Thanks

Ramesh

entry.title: Re: Need a step by step help create an API with APIM ,set...
entry.content:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-btp-api-proxy-to-work-with-basic-authentication-or-oauth-2-0/bc-p/14127270#M173850

Follow the steps, and check condition Strings to work for Basic or oAuth

 

Thanks

Ramesh

entry.title: Re: need help connecting Salesforce to SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Yes, you can connect a Salesforce connected app to SAP Build Apps, but it requires the correct configuration in SAP BTP.

entry.title: How to update a Role's model level privacy restrictions in SAC using an API
entry.content:
Hi Experts, we are looking into automating SAC Security. 

SAC tool provides import of users, so we can accommodate the new users, or updates to existing users to certain extent. 

Similarly, if we get import of teams updates, that would be great. Is there a way we can do this through an API, using a file upload, etc.

Also, if Role's model level privacy restrictions in SAC can be updated using an API, then all the aspects can be fully automated. 

Thanks & Best Regards,

SP

entry.title: 🚛 Unlocking the Power of Business AI in SAP Transportation Management – Start Here!
entry.content:
As logistics operations grow more complex and customer expectations continue to rise, the need for smarter, faster, and more intuitive solutions in transportation management has never been greater. SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM), part of SAP S/4HANA, is evolving to meet this demand—with Business AI at its core.

If you're exploring how artificial intelligence can create real business value in SAP TM, you’re in the right place.

This series of blog serves as the entry point to AI use cases that demonstrate how SAP is bringing intelligence to logistics execution and planning solutions. The blogs explains the use cases, configuration settings and helps you to test the scenarios in your own landscape.

✅ Use Case 1: AI-Assisted Goods Receipt Analysis

Blog link: Speed Up Check-ins – AI-Assisted Goods Receipt Analysis in SAP S/4HANA TM

Problem Addressed: Long truck waits times and manual, error-prone check-in procedures.

AI Value Delivered:

  • Uses generative AI to interpret unstructured documents such as delivery notes.
  • Matches incoming delivery documents with planned consignment orders.
  • Automatically flags discrepancies and prepares the system for quick, efficient goods receipt.

Business Impact:

  • Reduces truck idle and wait time at receiving gate.
  • Speeds up gate-in processes and inbound verification.
  • Minimizes human effort and errors during check-in.

💬 Use Case 2: Conversational Planning Using Generative AI

Blog link: SAP S/4HANA TM – Conversational Planning Using Generative AI (Step-by-Step)

Problem Addressed: Complex planning processes requiring manual interaction with the Transportation Cockpit.

AI Value Delivered:

  • Enables planners to use natural language commands (e.g., “Plan all refrigerated products to Berlin using the smallest available truck”).
  • Simplifies transportation planning without navigating detailed UIs or applying filters manually.
  • Connects user intent directly with planning actions through generative AI.

Business Impact:

  • Enhances planner productivity.
  • Reduces learning curve and system navigation time.
  • Makes planning more intuitive and accessible.

🎯 Why These Use Cases Matter

Both examples show that Business AI in SAP TM is not just theoretical—it's here and working. Whether it's streamlining execution or making planning smarter and more natural, these innovations are already delivering measurable benefits to logistics teams.

📌 What’s Next?

This is just the beginning. SAP’s vision for Transportation Management includes even more AI-driven scenarios.

Follow this space as we continue to explore how Business AI can elevate logistics operations and simplify the everyday work of planners, coordinators, and supply chain managers.

👉 Ready to dive deeper? Start with the two blogs above and watch this space for more!

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: There is no standard info structure for info record f...
entry.content: -It is possible with SARI but not with ME1A

entry.title: Re: What is the reorganization run and when does it take...
entry.content:
Hello @TS01 

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, when you set the deletion indicator for a condition record (like in the Purchasing Info Record), it doesn’t delete the record immediately. Instead, it’s marked for deletion and will be removed later by a scheduled system job called the Reorganisation Run.

Until that job runs, the same Material/Variant/Validity combination can't be reused.

Can You Trigger It Manually?

No — this run is automated in Public Cloud and not exposed to end users.

  • Wait for the next reorganisation run (usually daily)
  • Or create the same condition with a slightly different variant or validity period to continue testing

 

SAP Help Reference

  • SAP Help: Manage Purchasing Info Records
  • SAP Note: Deletion Indicator in Condition Records  if accessible via ONE Support Launchpad

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: need help connecting Salesforce to SAP Build Apps

entry.title: Re: The transaction ABUMN doesn"t have the input fiel...
entry.content:
Hi  @meerwu 

If sender asset doesn't have unit of measure or quantity, why you want to put quantity in ABUMN.

You can simple do partial transfer with required amount and don't put anything on quantity field. Later you can update quantity by going to AS02.

Please check if it works for you.

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: need help connecting Salesforce to SAP Build Apps

entry.title: Re: Asset Document Type (AA) Reversal
entry.content:
Hi @sachin_thombre 

You can  use AB08 for reversal of Asset transactions. Go to AB08 and put your asset number and company code and execute. You will see all transaction related to particular asset. Select transaction which you want to reverse and click on "Reverse" button at the top >>>Pop up screen will come with date and reason. Input detail and press enter. Asset reversal document will be posted.

Please check and confirm. This should work.

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Create Billing Plan for a Sales Order Item through a...
entry.content: Thanks @Lakshmipathi for this information but I am trying to upload a file containing only few columns like SO#, SO Item#, Billing Date and Billing Value. All these FMs internally contain the same logic where it is not able to fill the FLPT_NEW internal table with the required dataset like FPLTR, other amount columns, period, etc. I am looking for a FM that takes care of creating billing schedule for a SO Item like standard VA02 transaction does where we just enter the billing date and rest of the fields are derived automatically. BDC does the trick but I am not sure if that is the only possible solution

entry.title: Why Cosine Similarity > L2 Distance for RAG Systems (usually) [Part 1/3]
entry.content:
When building RAG systems, we face a key choice: Cosine Similarity or L2 Distance to compare vectors?

Here's how I think about it:

  • Cosine cares about direction (the angle between vectors), ignoring their length.
  • L2 cares about absolute distance, including magnitude differences.

Most text embedding models output normalized or near-normalized vectors, so Cosine better captures semantic similarity—focusing on meaning rather than size.

Real-world examples:

  • For semantic text search—like finding the most relevant articles or documents—Cosine Similarity shines because it focuses on the meaning, regardless of how "strong" or "long" the vector is.
  • But in drug discovery using molecular embeddings, L2 Distance can be crucial. Here, embeddings might encode both molecular structure (direction) and binding affinity or potency (magnitude). Two compounds could have similar structural patterns but vastly different effectiveness levels. Cosine similarity would treat a weak drug and a potent drug as identical if they have the same structure, while L2 distance correctly accounts for the potency difference—critical when you're looking for compounds that are both structurally similar AND similarly active.

The key insight? Ask yourself: does the magnitude (size) of your vectors carry meaningful information for your problem, or do you only care about their direction? Your answer should drive your metric choice.

Pro tip: For normalized text embeddings (most modern models), cosine similarity and dot product actually produce identical rankings—and dot product is computationally faster!

Curious : when have you preferred L2 over Cosine (or vice versa) in your RAG pipelines?

Bonus: Do you know how Cosine Similarity is calculated ?

For vectors A = [a₁, a₂, a₃] and B = [b₁, b₂, b₃]

Cosine Similarity = Dot Product ÷ (|A| × |B|), therefore cosine similarity is just normalized dot product.

|A| = √(a₁² + a₂² + a₃² ), |B| is similar and the Dot Product = Dot Product = (a₁ × b₁) + (a₂ × b₂) + (a₃ × b₃)

Suppose:

Vector A = [3, 4, 0]

Vector B = [1, 2, 2]

Dot Product = 3x1 + 4x2 + 0x2 = 3 + 8 + 0 = 11

|A| = √(9 + 16 + 0) = √(25) = 5,

|B| = √(1 + 4 + 4) = √(9) = 3

Cosine Similarity = 11 / (5 x 3) = 11/15 = 0.73

A score of 0.73 indicates strong similarity - these vectors are pointing in quite similar directions.

NOTE : Cosine similarity gives scores from -1 to +1, allowing you to rank results by relevance - highest scores = best matches.

Read the entire series here :

  • Part I - Why Cosine Similarity > L2 Distance for RAG Systems (usually) [Part 1/3]
  • Part II - How to Actually Interpret L2 Distance Results [Part 2/3]
  • Part III - The Production Reality: Why Cosine Similarity Often Wins Anyway [Part 3/3]

 

  •

entry.title: How to Actually Interpret L2 Distance Results [Part 2/3]
entry.content:
In my previous post on choosing between Cosine Similarity and L2 Distance for RAG systems, I focused on when to use each metric. But there's a critical issue I didn't address: how to interpret L2 Distance results.

Here's the problem: while Cosine Similarity gives you a clean -1 to +1 range, L2 Distance ranges from 0 to infinity—making it nearly impossible to judge similarity without proper context.

The Interpretation Challenge:

Using the same vectors from my previous post:

  • Vector A = [3, 4, 0]
  • Vector B = [1, 2, 2]

We calculated Cosine Similarity = 0.73 (strong similarity), but what about L2 Distance?

L2 Distance Calculation:

L2 Distance = √[(a₁ - b₁)² + (a₂ - b₂)² + (a₃ - b₃)²]
L2 Distance = √[(3-1)² + (4-2)² + (0-2)²] = √(4 + 4 + 4) = 3.46

But here's the question: Is 3.46 similar or different? The answer depends entirely on your data range.

Real-world context matters:

  • If your vectors represent pixel values (0-255 range): 3.46 = very similar
  • If they represent financial data in millions: 3.46 = practically identical
  • If they represent coordinates in a 0-50 range: need to calculate to know

The Solution: Normalize by Maximum Possible Distance

For our example in a 0-50 range:

  • Most different possible vectors: [0,0,0] and [50,50,50]
  • Maximum L2 Distance: √[(50-0)² + (50-0)² + (50-0)²] = 86.6
  • Relative distance: 3.46 ÷ 86.6 = 0.04 (4% of maximum distance)
  • Verdict: Very similar!

Universal Normalization Formula:

For any vector space with data ranging from min to max:

Maximum L2 Distance = √[d × (max - min)²]

Where d = number of dimensions (3 for our example)

Relative Distance = Actual L2 Distance ÷ Maximum L2 Distance

Quick Reference Scale:

  • 0-6% of max distance: Very similar
  • 6-17%: Similar
  • 17-35%: Moderately different
  • 35%+: Very different

Pro tip: This normalization gives you a 0-100% similarity scale that works regardless of your data range—making L2 Distance as interpretable as Cosine Similarity.

Next week: I'll cover what happens when you can't determine your maximum distance, and why this leads many production systems back to Cosine Similarity.

Read the entire series here :

  • Part I - Why Cosine Similarity > L2 Distance for RAG Systems (usually) [Part 1/3]
  • Part II - How to Actually Interpret L2 Distance Results [Part 2/3]
  • Part III - The Production Reality: Why Cosine Similarity Often Wins Anyway [Part 3/3]

entry.title: The Production Reality: Why Cosine Similarity Often Wins Anyway [Part 3/3]
entry.content:
In Part 2, I showed how to normalize L2 Distance for proper interpretation. But here's what happens when theory meets production reality.

The Challenge: When You Can't Determine Maximum Distance

While we always know our vector dimensions (384, 768, 1536, etc.), normalization still faces real-world challenges:

Problem 1: Theoretical vs. Actual Range

Example with a hypothetical model:

  • Model docs say: "768-dimensional vectors, values range [-1, 1]"
  • You calculate: Max L2 = √[768 × (1-(-1))²] = √[768 × 4] = 55.4
  • Reality check: Your actual embeddings range [-0.4, 0.3]
  • Actual max L2: √[768 × (0.3-(-0.4))²] = √[768 × 0.49] = 19.4
  • Your error: Using 55.4 instead of 19.4 = off by 185%!

Problem 2: Dynamic Datasets

  • New documents added daily → empirical maximum keeps changing
  • Document duplicates → many L2 distances = 0, skewing statistics
  • Content types evolve → data ranges shift over time

The Modern Model Advantage: Unit Vectors

Here's where it gets interesting. Many modern embedding models (including OpenAI) solve this by outputting unit vectors—vectors with magnitude = 1.

Unit vector example:

  • Vector A = [0.6, 0.8, 0]
  • Vector B = [0.8, 0.6, 0]
  • Check: |A| = √(0.6² + 0.8² + 0²) = √1 = 1 ✓ (unit vector)

For unit vectors:

  • Maximum L2 distance is always 2 (between opposite vectors like [1,0,0] and [-1,0,0])
  • L2 Distance = √[(0.6-0.8)² + (0.8-0.6)² + (0-0)²] = √(0.04 + 0.04) = 0.28
  • Normalization: 0.28 ÷ 2 = 0.14 = 14% of maximum = very similar

Comparing approaches with the same vectors:

  • Our Part 2 method (0-50 range): 3.46 ÷ 86.6 = 4% similarity
  • Unit vector method (if normalized): 0.28 ÷ 2 = 14% similarity
  • Cosine Similarity (from Part 1): 0.73 = 73% similarity

Same vectors, three different interpretations!

But here's the kicker: For unit vectors, L2 distance and cosine similarity produce identical rankings!

If vectors A and C are more similar than A and B using cosine similarity, they'll also be more similar using L2 distance. You get the exact same search results either way.

But Even This Has Drawbacks:

Even with unit vectors making normalization straightforward and rankings equivalent, operational complexity remains:

  • Need to verify your model outputs unit vectors
  • Mixed models in one system → different normalization rules
  • Model updates can change normalization assumptions

Alternative approaches for production:

  1. Fixed domain thresholds: Learn L2 ranges from initial dataset, keep stable
  2. Rolling window normalization: Use recent N documents for stability
  3. Just use Cosine Similarity: Self-normalizing, immune to dataset changes

This brings us full circle to my original post: For most RAG systems, Cosine Similarity wins not just because of semantic focus, but because of operational simplicity.

The final insight? L2 Distance normalization is mathematically sound and often feasible, but adds operational complexity. A score of 0.85 in Cosine Similarity means the same thing today, tomorrow, and after 10,000 new documents.

Choose based on your system's complexity tolerance and data characteristics, not just mathematical elegance.

 

Read the entire series here :

  • Part I - Why Cosine Similarity > L2 Distance for RAG Systems (usually) [Part 1/3]
  • Part II - How to Actually Interpret L2 Distance Results [Part 2/3]
  • Part III - The Production Reality: Why Cosine Similarity Often Wins Anyway [Part 3/3]
  •

entry.title: External Refurbishment work order cost calculation
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have refurbished a material from valuation type C(Defective) to valuation type B(refurbished).

B Refurbished Moving Average Price 3000 Stock 1

C  Defective Moving Average Price 1000 Stock 1

Price of service 500

Why the gain loss is showing as -1500 instead of 0. As the amount spent on refurbishment and the C condition material are both completely consumed in inventory at B condition?

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: GR/IR Clearing Open Items - Manual postings to GR/IR accounts aren't allowed.
entry.content:
GR/IR Clearing Open Items.

the GR/IR GL is made into non automatic posting account, within GL master. For the manual entry posting to clearing the GR/IR open item which are related to the previous year.

When I tried posting the manual entry to GR/IR account it is not allowing to make entry by showing the below error.

Using app Post General Journal Entries for GR/IR Clearing account Error Manual postings to GR/IR accounts aren't allowed.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GR/IR Clearing Open Items - Manual postings to GR/IR...
entry.content:
Changing the G/L Account master data cannot over write the rule No manual posting is possible with a GR/IR clearing account.  

See kba3094021 Manual postings to GR/IR accounts aren't allowed.

entry.title: Unable to find any active legal regulations for determination proc. ""
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are trying in use determination procedure ALRG03 DLR: Country/Country Group. We want to find all applicable legal regulations activated under country(ies) and country groups.

When testing, the check log states..Unable to find any active legal regulations for determination proc. "". Is there something additional that need to be done to use ALRG03?

Regards, Dean.

entry.title: Re: How can the ledger be configured to display the GST a...
entry.content:
Hello @TAMS1 

To display consolidated GST amounts per invoice in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, follow this approach:

 Options:

1. Standard Apps
Use apps like:

    • Display Billing Documents
    • Manage Journal Entries
    • G/L Line Items
      → Filter/group by GST tax codes (e.g., IGST, CGST, SGST)

2. Custom Analytical Query
If standard apps don’t meet your need:

    • Build a Custom CDS View (based on I_TaxAmount or I_BillingDocumentItemCube)
    • Use the Custom Analytical Queries app to show GST totals per invoice

3. Tax Code Setup
Ensure correct tax codes and jurisdiction logic are set up (India GST content is pre-delivered in Public Cloud).

SAP Help Links:

  • Manage Journal Entries – SAP Help
  • Custom Analytical Queries – SAP Help
  • Create Custom CDS View – SAP Help

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Read/Write Access SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @seamustaylor 

In SAP Analytics Cloud, product cost visibility is restricted using data access control on the product dimension. Use a Data Action to calculate and write only the aggregated total to a visible dummy product.

 SAP Help: Configure Data Access Control

SAP Help: Data Actions – Advanced Scripting

SAC Best Practices for Sensitive Data Modelling

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Blog 10: Importing PI content in Integration Flows
entry.content:
Hi Sujit,

I just came across your blog from 10 years ago.

Lot has changed since then.

However, i need to download the PI design objects - esp the message mappings so i can upload them in CPI iFlow. In the NWDS, i do not see the import PI content

Yes i can export the mappings as excel but then that format is not compatible for uploading in CPI package which looks for zip (i tried zipping the excel MM but that gives the error == Upload of the resource failed. The project must contain valid source folders.)

NWDS only exports the simple mappings but not complex mappings as it does not generate an excel for them

Can you please advice on how the message mappings can be exported and then uploaded in CPI Package for the iFlows?

Regards,

entry.title: SuccessFactors Role Based Permission Clean Up
entry.content:

Should we delete them or just rename them so they are not used in the future? Any guidance is greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

Lisa

entry.title: Missing tab in SOAMANAGER
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

We're currently activating our Inbound for EDI, and while we run the SOAMANAGER, we didn't see the "Application and Scenario Communcation Tab". 

Is there any updates or changes for this tab name in SOAMANAGER? or is there a guide to add this tab via authorization in SU01 to be visible this in SOAMANAGER?

BTW, currently we're using S4 HANA Private Cloud Edition 2020.

Thank you in advance!
-Jaspher

entry.title: Can I add an annotation to a standard SAP CDS view if it does not affect compatibility?
entry.content:
I am working on a task to add a new field and hide existing fields from an "Assigned to" search help in Fiori App F5104A (Perform Maintenance Jobs). I have extended the "Assigned To" search help view to add a new field for division. In addition, in order to hide existing fields, I need to add a metadata extension. However, I get an error in the metadata extension shown below.  When I attempt to add the annotation to the standard base view, I get the warnings shown below as well. According to my findings it seems that if I do not modify or remove any existing parameters, associations, name changes, then anything else should not affect incompatibility. Is it ok to add the annotation "@Metadata.allowExtensions: true" to the standard base view in order to proceed with the metadata extension? The standard CDS view that has been extended and annotated is I_PersWrkAgrmtSrchHelp. 

"Assigned To" field in Fiori App F5104A:

"Assigned To" search help with division added:

 

Error in metadata extension:

 

Warning in standard CDS view when attempting to add "@Metadata.allowExtensions: true".

4

Link to the release contract (Use System-Internally (C1)) mentioned in the above warning:

Release Contracts | ABAP Keyword Documentation

I would appreciate the feedback in order to help my team and I move forward with this.

 

Thank you!

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Unable to find any active legal regulations for deter...
entry.content:
Hello @dean_hinson2 

Below, i summarized the solution

Required Task

What to do

ALRG03 not finding legal regs

Check assignment in Manage Legal Control Settings

Legal regulation not active

Confirm it's scoped and assigned

Country/country group missing

Assign via Configure Your Solution (SSCUI)

Testing

Use Check Compliance app or simulate a transaction

Scope item required

Ensure 1Z3 is activated

 

SAP Help References

  • SAP Help – Legal Control (Public Cloud)
  • Scope Item 1Z3 – Legal Control

With kind regards

Chujma

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud infrastructure is moving to SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content: The migration process is underway. The plan is that customers will be migrated during the normal maintenance windows. Customers will need to wait their turn as we will be migrating based on a number of factors such as the data center provider and region, customer system size etc.

entry.title: Re: 🚀 Avoid Performance Pitfalls: Master Data Design Tips for SAP Convergent Charging

entry.title: Join Our Business Builders Roadshow | July 8 in Spanish!
entry.content:
We are thrilled to invite you to our Business Builders Roadshow, where we will be launching two new games on our platform!

Join us for our regional roadshow, hosted by SAP University Alliances and HEC Montréal on July 8th 17:00 CEST | 11:00 EDT in Spanish (Check out other dates and times here), where we will provide you with a hands-on preview of the new games:

  1. The Maple Heir, a business fundamentals scenario developed by Prof. Michael Bliemel,
  2. The Agent, a sports management scenario developed by Prof. Olivier Caya.

These games are perfect for students from school to university, making data visualization and data storytelling fun and easy to learn.

You’ll have the opportunity to play these games firsthand and experience the fun and learning they provide. You’ll also experience demos, regional faculty testimonials, and Q&A, providing a comprehensive taste of the new games.

Whether you are already familiar with Business Builders or are new to the community platform, this is a fantastic opportunity to discover how a gamified learning experience can help enhance classroom learning and develop SAP Analytics Cloud skills. By integrating Business Builders into your curriculum, you will be giving your students the chance to tackle real-world business challenges, empowering them to stand out in the job market.

And the best: Business Builders is free for educational use.

Register now and let’s shape the future of learning together!

See you there!

entry.title: Re: "PD Where Used" Button – A Chrome Extension for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content: This is a great feature, would be useful where the address is static, for dynamic addresses this option would not work, such as determining through property.

entry.title: SAP HANA Backup to tape with Veeam-Plug-In
entry.content:
Sehr geehrte Damen und PERSON_NAME,

 

wir möchten unsere SAP HANA-DB künftig über die Backint-Schnittstelle und dem Veeam-Plug-In für SAP HANA sichern.
Für unser Test-System wurde der Prozess mittlerweile auch schon eingerichtet und funktioniert auch problemlos.

 

Aktuell wird das Backup über einen backup-Job in das lokale Veeam-Repository auf dem Backup-Server geschrieben.
Wir möchten das Backup künftig in einem zweiten Step jedoch auch noch auf ein Tape speichern.

 

Hierfür stehen gem. unseren Informationen folgende Optionen zu Verfügung: 

 

(1) Files to tape

 

(2) Backups to tape:

 

Da man hier die SAP-Backup-Jobs aber nicht auswählen kann, müsste man sich in der Job-Konfiguration für Variante „Backup Repositories“ auswählen. Dies setzt dann voraus, dass man die SAP HANA Jobs auf ein separates Repository laufen lässt und nur dieses sichert.

 

Können Sie uns sagen, welche Option gem. SAP die bessere wäre?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Backup to tape with Veeam-Plug-In
entry.content: SAP HANA backups to tape can be supported via third-party backup tools that are certified to use the Backint for SAP HANA interface. These tools can integrate with SAP HANA to perform backup and recovery operations, including transferring backups to tape. It is essential that the third-party tool's API implementation is certified by SAP and that you have a support contract with the tool vendor.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance order display blank screen in SAP Service and Asset Manager
entry.content: Hi Muhammad, Debug attached

entry.title: Re: Unable to find any active legal regulations for deter...
entry.content: Hello Chujma, The LRREG is configured with Country Group and ExpCtl. = 4. Manage Legal Control Settings is not in GTSE4H. Control Settings for Legal Control is in GTSE4H and I don't see anything that would cause the issue I am encountering. Country Group is not missing. SSCUI transaction doers not exist in GTSE4H. Also, we are not on the Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Tutorial: Save and display text field more than 255 characters from Table Maintenance (SM30)
entry.content:
Hi arnabdatta3,

thank you for sharing this solution. I'm encountering an issue when inserting new rows: after some investigation, I found out that the vim_status for new rows should be neuer instead of aendern.

I slightly modified the code to handle this case as follows:

IF <fs_line_struc>-vim_action NE neuer.
  <fs_line_struc>-vim_action = aendern.
ENDIF.

However, after saving and navigating back, the system still prompts me to save again, as if the previous save wasn't recognized.

Please, let me now if you have any suggestions on how to fix this.

entry.title: Re: Unable to find any active legal regulations for deter...
entry.content: Hello Chujma, Also to cover bases, the legal regulations has both country group and individual coutnries assigned.

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Ariba SLP with S/4HANA Public Cloud Using SAP Master Data Integration
entry.content:
Good to see a blog on new approach Karthikeyan_C. Appreciate it.

I have a question on Partition Supplier Replication from S4 Cloud to Ariba Buying. Not sure if you can suggest some process.

My scenario is we don't have SAP Variant Realms, so cannot go with this approach of SLP integration with S4 Cloud. Planning to use CIG+CPI and consume SAP_COM_0008 scope for Common Suppliers replication (Hope it will work). How about Partition Supplier replication ? Since we don't have any CIG Addon to generate the partition data, i believe we should go with Customization on S4 Cloud to generate these partition files using Developer 

Any suggestions on process or best approach for generating Partition data from S4 Cloud to Ariba B&I. 

Appreciate any suggestions.

entry.title: Re: Can I add an annotation to a standard SAP CDS view if...
entry.content:
Is it ok to add the annotation "@Metadata.allowExtensions: true" to the standard base view in order to proceed with the metadata extension? 

No. It's a change in a standard artifact. 

Have you tried using UI Adaptation to show and hide other fields? It seems feasible based on your request.

entry.title: Re: Report on suppliers' bank data
entry.content:
Hello AlicjaK. Welcome to the SAP Community. Your question is valid - but it lacks important details and it's assigned to the wrong tag. This is not a SAPUI5 question.

I highly suggest you to spend some time reading the following blog post: 
https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-blog-posts/asking-good-questions-in-the-scn-discussion-spaces-will-help-you-get-good/ba-p/12880734

Just to give some insights, I would add in your question:

- Which system and version you are using
- Where and how you have done your research
- Any tables/CDS views that might be helpful containing the data you need

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Test Case manual sequencing reorg fun...
entry.content: The benefit of Cloud at work here -> this enhancement has been delivered. There is now a means to indicate a sequence. KBA 3610413 has been updated to reflect this.

entry.title: Re: Condition Type GWLB for Intrastat
entry.content: Hi All, At the moment, we have the following customization in the system: Level Counter KArt Description From To Manu Obl Stat D ZwiSu 70 0 GRWR Border Crossing Value 20 X 75 0 GWLB Limit Value LB-Comp. X 79 0 Sum of Border Crossing 70 78 X C Values. The condition GRWR and the subtotal have the requirement '008'. The condition GWLB has the requirement '54' and the formula '60'. Level 79 has been assigned subtotal C so that only the total sum is passed as the border crossing value. How can I have the GRWR condition automatically included in an order and not select it manually?

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required: Restricting Receipt Stock Usage...
entry.content:
@Kiransonawane  , Can you share a bit more info?

From what I understand, here's a possible approach:
You can create the production order in your WIP warehouse, and once production is done and the order is closed, just transfer the finished goods to your FG warehouse. In the meantime, block your sales users from making any transactions directly from the WIP warehouse.

entry.title: Re: 🇲🇽 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Mexico City, Mexico)
entry.content:
Bonjour, les gars.
I've registered for the SAP CodeJam event. I'm excited to participate in this session!

entry.title: Is there a BTP service or API to track user login/logout times and session duration?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a project in SAP BTP and would like to know if there is any built-in service or API that can provide information such as:

  • When a user logged in

  • When they logged out

  • How long they stayed logged in (session duration)

This would help us monitor usage patterns and possibly audit access times.

Is there a service (like Audit Log, Identity Authentication, or another BTP capability) that offers this kind of data? If so, how can it be accessed?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: TDS LINE ITEM NOT PICKING IN J1INCHLN
entry.content: Hi Rudra did you get any answer I am also facing same issue

entry.title: ORACLE_BASE to use when switching DB software home from SAP Classic to Oracle standard
entry.content:
I have installed a new /oracle/RDBMS/19.0.0 shared home on server on which an existing SAP classic user installation was done under /oracle/C11/19.0.0

Note 1521371 suggests the shared ORACLE_BASE should be /oracle/RDBMS on a shared home, but in Note 1915317, Step 7 "Chceck Oracle environment for consistency suggests that the ORACLE_BASE must be the same when logged in as the ora<sid> or oracle users.  

which is correct?

I actually installed the new shared home with ORACLE_BASE=/oracle, which is how we originally did the software install under the SAP Classic user setup.  Even though ORACLE_BASE under the SAP classic user (oraq11) was specified as /oracle, when logged in as the oraq11 user the ORACLE_BASE=/oracle/Q11.  So I thought I'd be ok to do the same.

Now I'm getting permissions issues when I try to run SBP bundle patching for the first time because it can't write to the cfgtoollogs directory.  It is set with permission 755.  On some other non-SAP oracle installations that I have the cfgtoollogs directory has 775.

I'm really confused as to which path to follow and what is expected, as SAP installations are different from a typical Oracle installation with which I'm familiar.

entry.title: Re: Warehouse Transfers
entry.content:
Hi @FilipeCorreia ,

Let’s say you want to transfer an item from Warehouse A to Warehouse B.
You can do a Goods Issue from Warehouse A using the actual cost, and then create a Goods Receipt in Warehouse B with the actual cost + any landed costs you want to apply.

Let me know if this works for you!

entry.title: Re: How to work with exemptions using ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud during development in an
entry.content:
Hi, @OlgaDolinskaja,

 In  app approve ATC Exemptions, the buttons aprove, reject and delete is enabled. Can you a help?

entry.title: Re: Limit sales order partner function number - taken onl...
entry.content:
Hi , In your partner detemination procedure, there is an option called "Not modifiable". By selecting  this option, the partner function becomes non-editable in the sales order. It will be automatically copied from the customer master and cannot be changed in the sales order . Please find screen shot in below.

.  Please check this option and let us know if you still see an issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 HANA approval using queries
entry.content:
Hi @user2001 ,

Did you check the Approval Status Report? It should list all the approval stages for the specific document. Let me know what you see there!

entry.title: Re: Can I add an annotation to a standard SAP CDS view if...
entry.content:
Hi Fabio,

Thank you for your response. We hoped it would be that easy but not the case here unfortunately.

Rearranging the toolbar is not allowed.  It only allows us to add a fragment, or extend with a controller. 

 

Do you have any other options?

Thank you!

Patrick

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP API Proxy to Work with Basic Authentication or OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
Hi Ramesh,

I have a iflow endpoint which can be accessed with clientid and clientsecret  which we get from instance service key , if we are using the endpoint from postman or some other system, BUT I dont want to give clientid and clientsecret for Authentication , I want to create my own username and password and give to vendors or system to be accessed.
how can achieve that?
Please guide, As I am new to APIM.

Thanks,

Ikram

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Topics
entry.content: @Josh90 @That is not a topic we plan to add at this time

entry.title: Where do I find SuccessFactors Learning Admin job aids (ie. how to create a curricula)?
entry.content: I would like to find step by step instructions on how to tackle LMS Admin tasks. I can only seem to find very high level information and it's never in the same place twice.  I've been searching for over an hour with no luck.

entry.title: Business transformation management: Where do I start?
entry.content:
In the Experience Center at SAP Sapphire in Orlando and Madrid, we presented our full set of business transformation management capabilities. We did it by walking attendees through a scenario in which a company wanted to take full advantage of the SAP Business Suite, SAP BDC, and SAP Business AI by transforming a core business process. 

In the scenario we laid out, the company starts by using the Business AI embedded in SAP Signavio Transformation Advisor to recommend process improvements. Subsequently, through Signavio’s integration with SAP LeanIX, the company discovers that phasing in a successor application to support the transformed processes would also be beneficial.  

As the analysis progresses, the company decides on a plan of action, leveraging Signavio’s analysis and mining solutions to quantify the value they seek to realize in their transformation. They then take advantage of Signavio’s transformation management capabilities to collaboratively initiate, plan, manage, and execute their planned initiatives. 

Since their envisioned transformation involves changes to their enterprise architecture, including extending SAP S/4HANA with Ariba, the company plans their enterprise architecture roadmap in LeanIX. They also document this architectural decision there and record the changes by synchronizing everything with SAP Cloud ALM.  

Finally, to facilitate employee enablement, the company uses WalkMe to track and analyze adoption as well as to provide employees with in-app guidance.   

The power of an integrated toolchain for business transformation management 

As the scenario illustrates, an integrated toolchain makes business transformation a whole lot easier. It makes it possible to smoothly move from insight to planning to action to analysis, drilling down into the most relevant details and connecting those details to the broader business context.  

For example, the toolchain described can reveal manual steps in key processes, help you establish that the culprit is a poor functional fit between the process and the underlying application, and then plan for its replacement. 

Once you’ve phased it in, you can manage adoption of that application while also confirming, on the process level, that your changes are really delivering the value your initial analysis suggested you could achieve. 

In both Orlando and Madrid, customers, prospects, and partners (and even some of our SAP colleagues!) were uniformly enthusiastic when we showed them what our business transformation management toolset, supported by AI from start to finish, could do. 

As SAP LeanIX product marketer, Seth Lippincott put it, “There was no ambiguity in the responses we got. No one said, ‘Meh.’ They all wanted to know more.” 

Which is not to say that no one had questions. In fact, quite the opposite was true. In fact, there was one question that came up again and again: “Where do I start?” 

The answer to that question, as with so many questions in the complex world of enterprise software and leading-edge technology, is: It depends! 

Option 1: Start with a focus on your enterprise architecture 

So, what does the answer to this question depend on? Well, it depends first and foremost on your starting point.  

Let’s say your situation is this. You are relatively new to the SAP universe, and you just purchased SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. You want to transform your business by streamlining your business processes and modernizing the underlying enterprise architecture. In this scenario, it will probably take less time to create an inventory of your applications and, using the reference architecture content in LeanIX, map these applications to your business capabilities.  

That simple step can be quite revealing, showing you quickly where you have redundancies (i.e., more than one application supporting the same business capability), where you have gaps (unsupported capabilities), and where, for example, what applications you have on premise and what you have in the cloud.  

With this information, supported where possible by WalkMe’s insights into application usage, you can begin rationalizing your landscape, retiring or replacing applications that deliver no value, and ensuring future support for your critical business capabilities.  

The beauty of this approach? You can use the budget freed up through rationalization to fund your transformation efforts going forward.  

It also means that, as your business process management practice matures, you can design your to-be process landscape around a consolidated and optimized set of applications.  

That it’s easier to seamlessly enable your employees on a simplified application landscape? Icing on the cake.  

Option 2: Start with a focus on process excellence 

The scenario we presented in the Experience Center started with sourcing and procurement processes. One good reason for this was that the customer in the story had already embarked on their RISE with SAP journey. Based on that, we could assume that the customer was an established SAP ERP customer and already had key solutions from LeanIX, Signavio, and WalkMe in place.  

In many ways, your business processes are your business; they are the way you do things. If you want to bring costs down or accelerate time to market, it just makes sense to start with this operational reality–in other words, to dig into what’s actually happening and focus on what you could improve, optimize, and transform.  

Process transformation can deliver substantial, measurable impact. It also represents the centerpiece of any true business transformation, which, after all, means “changing the way you do business.” For this reason, starting with processes can provide you with a very practical lens through which to view things when you then turn to the transformation of your enterprise architecture.  

If the search for ways to optimize your processes uncovers the need for a new application, for example, you automatically have the business context and rationale for it. This then allows you to make the case and even quantify the value of the recommended architectural change. 

Option 3: Start wherever you can 

As we said above, where you start with transformation depends. It depends on what type of transformation you want to pursue. It depends on the solutions you have in place. And it depends on your organization’s capacity for transformation, or, putting it another way, how much transformation your organization is capable of.  

If your organization has developed a mature business process management practice, if you have an established enterprise architecture practice, if digital adoption is built into the way you use technology, then you have what we call a robust transformation capability. If that’s the case, you can start wherever you think you can generate the most value. 

That might mean that you start in several places at once. You can work on transforming business processes, modernizing your architecture, and enabling your entire organization to incorporate AI in every workflow. So long as the solutions you use are well integrated, and you coordinate your plans and roadmaps, you can be opportunistic when it comes to transformation.  

The challenge at this level of maturity is not figuring out where to start, but how best to orchestrate your efforts so they reinforce and amplify each other.  

When to start, not where to start, is the real question 

Where to start your transformation efforts can depend on myriad factors, as we have shown. 

When to start, however, only depends on one thing: Will successful transformation make your business better, more innovative, more competitive, more valuable?  

If the answer is yes, start today.
